title: crash_info.c:35-37 count initialization before magic check creates race window
description:
Completeness check: Round 1-2 missed subtle ordering issue in crash_info.

WHAT: In crash_info_store(), the code first checks if magic is invalid and sets count=0, then sets magic. There's a timing window where:
1. crash_info.count = 0 is written
2. Interrupt occurs, system resets
3. On next boot, crash_info.magic is still garbage
4. count value is lost (was set to 0 but magic never confirmed it)

CODE:
```c
if (crash_info.magic != CRASH_MAGIC) {
    crash_info.count = 0;  // Write happens...
}
crash_info.magic = CRASH_MAGIC;  // ...but this might never execute
```

WHY BUG: On Cortex-M, each 32-bit write is atomic, but there's no guarantee both writes complete before an interrupt/reset. The count field could be written (zeroing previous crash count) while magic remains invalid, losing crash history.

IMPACT: Low - the primary purpose (recording crash reason/location/extra) is unaffected. Only the cumulative count across boots could be lost.

FIX: Set magic BEFORE modifying count, or use a two-phase commit:
```c
if (crash_info.magic != CRASH_MAGIC) {
    crash_info.magic = CRASH_MAGIC;  // First, claim the struct
    crash_info.count = 0;            // Then, initialize fields
}
```
close_reason: Fixed crash_info_store initialization ordering so CRASH_MAGIC is written before resetting retained crash count, and verified with focused object compile plus full existing bridge build
